description: Add group support to built-in browser tools, matching the existing group feature in the plugin SDK. Browser tools will be assigned to logical groups (Tabs, Page Interaction, Page Inspection, Storage & Cookies, Network, Extension, Plugins) and the side panel will render them with grouped section headers and per-group toggle switches, identical to how plugin tools render groups.

userStories.title: Add group field to BrowserToolDefinition, CachedBrowserTool, BrowserToolMeta, and ConfigStateBrowserTool
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the browser tool type definitions to include an optional group field so that browser tools can be assigned to logical groups like plugin tools are.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolDefinition interface in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/definition.ts has an optional group?: string field with a JSDoc comment
CachedBrowserTool interface in platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts has an optional group?: string field
BrowserToolMeta interface in platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts has a readonly group?: string field
ConfigStateBrowserTool interface in platform/shared/src/index.ts has an optional group?: string field with a JSDoc comment
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Four type definitions need the group field added:

1. `platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/definition.ts` line 18 — `BrowserToolDefinition` interface: add `group?: string` after the `icon` field (line 22). Follow the same JSDoc pattern as `icon`.

2. `platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts` line 152 — `CachedBrowserTool` interface: add `group?: string` after the `icon` field. This is the cached version used at runtime.

3. `platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts` line 8 — `BrowserToolMeta` interface: add `readonly group?: string` after the `icon` field. NOTE: This file is auto-generated but the interface definition needs updating in the generator script too (US-003 handles that). For this story, update both the generated file AND the interface.

4. `platform/shared/src/index.ts` line 304 — `ConfigStateBrowserTool` interface: add `group?: string` after the `icon` field. This is the wire type sent to the extension side panel.

All four additions are optional fields with `?` — no existing code breaks.

userStories.title: Assign groups to all browser tool definitions
userStories.description: As a user viewing the side panel, I want browser tools organized into logical groups so that I can find and manage related tools together.
acceptanceCriteria: Every browser tool definition file in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/ has a group field set
Tab management tools (browser_list_tabs, browser_open_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_navigate_tab, browser_focus_tab, browser_get_tab_info) have group: 'Tabs'

Page interaction tools (browser_click_element, browser_type_text, browser_select_option, browser_press_key, browser_scroll, browser_hover_element, browser_handle_dialog) have group: 'Page Interaction'

Page inspection tools (browser_screenshot_tab, browser_get_tab_content, browser_get_page_html, browser_query_elements, browser_wait_for_element, browser_execute_script, browser_get_console_logs, browser_clear_console_logs, browser_list_resources, browser_get_resource_content) have group: 'Page Inspection'
Storage and cookie tools (browser_get_storage, browser_get_cookies, browser_set_cookie, browser_delete_cookies) have group: 'Storage & Cookies'
Network tools (browser_enable_network_capture, browser_get_network_requests, browser_get_websocket_frames, browser_export_har, browser_disable_network_capture) have group: 'Network'
Extension tools (extension_reload, extension_get_state, extension_get_logs, extension_get_side_panel, extension_check_adapter, extension_force_reconnect) have group: 'Extension'
Plugin tools (plugin_list_tabs, plugin_analyze_site) have group: 'Plugins'
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Add a `group` field to each `defineBrowserTool()` call. There are ~36 browser tool files in `platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/`. Each file exports a single tool via `defineBrowserTool({ name, description, icon, input, handler })`. Add `group: '<GroupName>'` to each object literal.

Group assignments:
- **Tabs**: list-tabs.ts, open-tab.ts, close-tab.ts, navigate-tab.ts, focus-tab.ts, get-tab-info.ts
- **Page Interaction**: click-element.ts, type-text.ts, select-option.ts, press-key.ts, scroll.ts, hover-element.ts, handle-dialog.ts
- **Page Inspection**: screenshot-tab.ts, get-tab-content.ts, get-page-html.ts, query-elements.ts, wait-for-element.ts, execute-script.ts, get-console-logs.ts, clear-console-logs.ts, list-resources.ts, get-resource-content.ts
- **Storage & Cookies**: get-storage.ts, get-cookies.ts, set-cookie.ts, delete-cookies.ts
- **Network**: enable-network-capture.ts, get-network-requests.ts, get-websocket-frames.ts, export-har.ts, disable-network-capture.ts
- **Extension**: reload-extension.ts, extension-get-state.ts, extension-get-logs.ts, extension-get-side-panel.ts, extension-check-adapter.ts, extension-force-reconnect.ts
- **Plugins**: plugin-list-tabs.ts, analyze-site.ts

Place the `group` field after `icon` (or after `description` if no `icon`) for consistency.

userStories.title: Thread group through caching, serialization, and catalog generation
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the group field to flow through the full stack (caching, extension serialization, catalog generation) so that the side panel receives group data for browser tools.
acceptanceCriteria: rebuildCachedBrowserTools in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts includes group in the CachedBrowserTool it produces (conditionally, only when present)
buildConfigStatePayload in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts includes group in the browser tool objects sent to the extension (conditionally, only when present)

The catalog generator script (scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts) extracts the group field from each tool definition and includes it in the generated BrowserToolMeta entries (conditionally, only when present)
The catalog generator updates the BrowserToolMeta interface in the generated file to include readonly group?: string
The barrel validation in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts validates group matches between tool definitions and BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG entries
npm run generate:browser-tools-catalog produces a catalog with group fields on all entries
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Five locations need changes to thread the group field through the stack:

1. **`platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts` line 87 — `rebuildCachedBrowserTools`**: Add `...(bt.group ? { group: bt.group } : {})` to the CachedBrowserTool object literal (after the icon spread on line 94). Follow the same conditional-inclusion pattern used for `icon`.

2. **`platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts` line 310 — `buildConfigStatePayload`**: Add `...(ct.group ? { group: ct.group } : {})` to the browser tool map callback (after the icon spread on line 317). Follow the same pattern.

3. **`scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts` line 41**: Add `group: t.group` to the extracted metadata (after `icon`). Then in the generation loop (line 67), add a conditional line for group: `if (entry.group) lines.push(\`    group: \${singleQuote(entry.group)},\`);`. Also update the generated BrowserToolMeta interface (line 58-62) to include `readonly group?: string`.

4. **`platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts` line 99 — barrel validation**: Add a group check alongside the existing description and icon checks. Compare `tool.group` with `catalogEntry.group` — both may be undefined, so use `tool.group ?? undefined` vs `catalogEntry.group ?? undefined` for the comparison. Throw a mismatch error suggesting to run the catalog generator.

5. After making all changes, run `npm run generate:browser-tools-catalog` to regenerate the catalog file with group data from the tool definitions in US-002.

IMPORTANT: The conditional-inclusion pattern `...(x ? { group: x } : {})` is used throughout the codebase for optional fields. Always use this pattern — never set `group: undefined`.

userStories.title: Render browser tools with grouped sections in the side panel BrowserToolsCard
userStories.description: As a user viewing the side panel, I want browser tools displayed in grouped sections with headers and per-group toggle switches so that I can easily find and manage related browser tools, matching the grouped display used for plugin tools.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolsCard groups browser tools by their group field using the same grouping logic as PluginCard (first-seen order, ungrouped tools under 'Other' at the end)

Each group renders a section header with the group name in uppercase tracking-wider text, styled with the same classes as PluginCard's group headers (flex items-center gap-2 border-border border-b bg-muted/20 px-3 py-1, font-head text-muted-foreground text-xs uppercase tracking-wider)
Each group header has a Switch toggle that enables/disables all tools in that group
Group toggle calls setBrowserToolEnabled for each tool in the group, with optimistic UI update and error rollback (same pattern as existing handleToggleTool)
Tool filter still works correctly — filters within groups and hides empty groups
When no tools have a group field (backward compatibility), renders the flat list as before
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
The grouping logic should closely follow `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx` lines 111-134 which implement the same feature for plugin tools. Key implementation details: 1. **Group computation**: After filtering tools by `toolFilter`, compute groups from `visibleTools`. Check if any tool has a `group` field (`visibleTools.some(t => t.group)`). If so, build a `Map` preserving first-seen order. Tools without a group go under 'Other', moved to end. 2. **Group toggle handler**: Create a `handleToggleGroup(groupTools: BrowserToolState[], checked: boolean)` function. It should: - Increment `toggleCounter.current` - Optimistically update all tools in the group via `onToolsChange` - Call `setBrowserToolEnabled` for each tool in the group (fire all requests in parallel) - On any failure, rollback via `preToggleRef.current` Note: Unlike plugin tools which have a batch `setToolsEnabled` API, browser tools use individual `setBrowserToolEnabled(toolName, enabled)` calls. The handler must call this for each tool in the group. 3. **Rendering**: Replace the flat `visibleTools.map(tool => )` in the `Accordion.Content` (line 160) with conditional group rendering: - If `hasAnyGroup`, render each group with a header div and per-group Switch, then ToolRows for each tool in the group - If no groups, render the flat list as before - The group header style: `
` with `{group.name}` and a ` t.enabled)} onCheckedChange={...} />` 4. **Filter interaction**: When `toolFilter` is active, groups with no matching tools should not render. The filter already produces `visibleTools` — grouping operates on `visibleTools`, so empty groups are naturally excluded. 5. The `BrowserToolState` type is `ConfigStateBrowserTool` from shared, which now has the optional `group?: string` field (added in US-001). The side panel receives this via the `config.getState` WebSocket response. 6. Also check `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` — it may need to preserve the `group` field when parsing browser tool data from the server, similar to how it preserves `group` for plugin tools (line 208). Search for where `ConfigStateBrowserTool` objects are constructed from wire messages and ensure `group` is included. Reference: See PluginCard.tsx lines 88-104 for the group toggle handler pattern and lines 227-253 for the group rendering JSX.
